MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON MAY-13-2010 IN 206, PLANITIFF'S MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL AND/OR SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS HEARD. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT SETS A TRIAL DATE. THE TRIAL DATE OF 5/24/10 IS CONTINUED TO 1/10/2011 IN DEPT. 206 AT 9:30 AM. THE CURRENT SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WILL BE VACATED, AND THE COURT WILL SEND NOTICE OF THE NEW SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE. THE THREE DEPOSITIONS NOTICED BY PLAINTIFF WILL BE CONDUCTED. THE DEADLINE FOR THE EXCHANGE OF DEMAND OF EXPERT WITNESSES WILL TRACK THE NEW TRIAL DATE. ALL OTHER DISCOVERY IS CLOSED AS OF THE OLD TRIAL DATE WITH THE EXCEPTION IF THERE'S ANY DEMANDS FOR DISCOVERY THAT'S OUTSTANDING THAT HAVE NOT BEEN FULFILLED WILL TRACK THE NEW TRIAL DATE. THERE'S NO MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER AND APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANT KPMG LLP MOTION TO COMPEL MENTAL EXAMINATION OF PLTF SARIA NADEEM IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. (1) THE MENTAL EXAMINATION'S SCOPE WILL BE PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGED EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, BOTH ITS CAUSES AND EFFECTS. THE EXAMINATION MAY BE AUDIOTAPED, BUT IS NOT TO BE VIDEOTAPED. (2) A PERSONALITY DISORDER (IF ANY EXISTS) COULD CONTRIBUTE TO PLAINTIFF'S DISTRESS; THUS, THE EXPERTS ARE NOT EXCLUDED FROM EXAMINATION IN THAT AREA. LIKEWISE, THE FACTS OF THE CASE ARE CLEARLY RELEVANT TO PLAINTIFF'S DISTRESS CLAIMS. (3) WITHIN 14 DAYS, DEFENDANT KPMG IS TO PROVIDE SPECIFICATIONS OF THE "DIAGNOSTIC TESTS AND PROCEDURES" FOR THE EXAMINATIONS, AS REQUIRED BY CCP 2032.320. JUDGE RICHARD B. ULMER, JR. Off Calendar Jun-24-2011 Continued To Jul-13-11 At 03:00 P.M. In Dept 611
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on the following motions filed by plaintiff Saria Nadeem:

(1) Motion to obtain confidential juror information;
(2) Motion for a new trial;
(3) Motion to tax costs and
(4) Motion for judgment notwithstanding the verdict.

Dept. 611, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Joe Vickstein, #4780.

Plaintiff Saria Nadeem was present in pro per, placed under oath and testified.
John Henning III, Esq. specially appeared with Ms. Nadeem.
Stuart Tochner, Esq. appeared for defendant KPMG, LLP.

After hearing oral argument by plaintiff and defense counsel, and good cause appearing, the Court rules as follows:

All four of plaintiff's motions are DENIED. Counsel for defendant shall prepare the Order After Hearing.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Mental Examination Of Pltf Saria Nadeem; Memorandum Of P/A In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Feb-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Erica Rocush, Atty. for defendant and moving party: KPMG, LLP
No appearances made by Plantiff.

RULING: RULING AFTER Hearing DEFENDANT KPMG LLP Motion To Compel Mental Examination Of Pltf Saria Nadeem is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. (1) The mental examination's scope will be Plaintiff's alleged emotional distress, both its causes and effects. The examination may be audiotaped, but is not to be videotaped. (2) A personality disorder (if any exists) could contribute to Plaintiff's distress; thus, the experts are not excluded from examination in that area. Likewise, the facts of the case are clearly relevant to Plaintiff's distress claims. (3) Within 14 days, Defendant KPMG is to provide specifications of the "diagnostic tests and procedures" for the examinations, as required by CCP 2032.320. JUDGE RICHARD B. ULMER, JR. Reporter: Ramona Reinhardt, CSR# 2677
